headline: OpenAI, Google, Microsoft back AI literacy bill for schools
body: OpenAI, Google, and Microsoft are backing legislation to fund AI literacy programs in schools, signaling major tech players' commitment to workforce development. For healthcare AI specifically, this matters because the clinical workforce will need foundational AI competency to effectively implement and validate AI tools in EHR systems and clinical workflows. As AI literacy becomes a baseline expectation rather than specialized knowledge, healthcare organizations should prepare for a talent pipeline with better AI fundamentals, reducing training friction for clinicians adopting decision-support systems. This also shapes the consulting landscape: vendors and health systems can expect healthcare workers to arrive with better understanding of AI capabilities and limitations, changing how organizations approach change management and clinical governance around AI deployment.
